title: gnaphalium canescens subsp. thermale
intro: Gnaphalium canescens subsp. thermale, also known as the Hot Springs Cudweed, is a subspecies of the Gnaphalium canescens plant, a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and protect the skin. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, calming skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Gnaphalium canescens subsp. thermale can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or salve, making it a versatile remedy for various skin issues and health concerns.
